hypotheses.report:
Investigation Report: b63a0168
============================================================
Status: active  —  Created 2026-03-24

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that vix spike above 30 spy long events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when vix closes above 30 (first close in 30-day cluster), buy spy at next open
and hold 20 trading days. vix>30 signals extreme fear that systematically
mean-reverts over the following 3-4 weeks,
then SPY should rise by approximately 1.69% within 20 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Extreme fear pricing (VIX>30) overshoots fair
volatility through forced selling by leveraged funds (margin calls, forced
deleveraging, risk-parity rebalancing), creating a predictable mean-reversion
opportunity as institutional buyers re-enter at cheaper prices.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Leveraged retail/institutional funds face margin
calls during VIX>30 episodes. Risk-parity funds reduce equity exposure
mechanically. Once deleveraging completes, long-term allocators (pension
funds, sovereign wealth) deploy cash at perceived discounts.
  - Transmission channel: VIX>30 only occurs when selling pressure has already
been extreme. Mechanical exhaustion of forced selling combined with systematic
buy-at-dip mandates creates predictable mean reversion. 30-day clustering
captures only the FIRST spike.
  - Academic reference: Whaley (2000) VIX as investor fear gauge, Journal of
Portfolio Management. CBOE VIX White Paper. N=54 events 2000-2026: 5d
mean=+1.19% p=0.012, 20d mean=+1.69% p=0.045. Both horizons at p<0.05 passes
multiple testing.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 54 historical events across 1 stocks (SPY). All returns
are measured as abnormal returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from
broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: SPY index has inherent survivorship bias (failing
companies removed). This affects the level of SPY returns not the
timing/cyclical nature of our signal.

Selection bias control: 30-day clustering is conservative — we take only the
FIRST close above 30 in each 30-day window.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  broad market direction: Tested across bull (2003, 2009, 2019, 2021,
  2023-2024), bear (2001-2002, 2008, 2022), and recovery markets. Effect
  persists in all regimes.
  vix level: By definition VIX>30 at entry. Effect holds across VIX 30-80
  range.
  sector trend: SPY IS the market benchmark. No sector bias by construction.
  survivorship bias: SPY index has inherent survivorship bias but affects
  level not timing of signal.
  selection bias: 30-day clustering is conservative. Takes only first event in
  cluster.
  event timing: Entry at open of day after VIX close>30. VIX data available at
  market close.
  market regime: Signal holds across all market regimes 2000-2026. Modern era
  (2015+) shows strongest validation (20d: +2.92%, dir=79%).

3. Success Criteria (locked before testing)
------------------------------------------------------------
Valid if: SPY abnormal return (vs nothing, SPY IS benchmark so vs T-bill rate)
> +1.0% over 20 trading days from entry date. Secondary: direction > 50%.
Based on N=54 historical VIX>30 spikes, avg 20d return +1.69%, consistency
66.7%. Given pre-sold regime (SPY -7.3% pre-Liberation Day), consider valid if
return > 0% (positive abnormal) over 20d.

Confidence: 6/10  |  Pre-registration: 288e37f0e621375a

4. Outcome
------------------------------------------------------------
  Entry:    2026-03-30 at $639.74
  Deadline: 2026-04-27
  Stop:     8.0%

Trade is active. No outcome yet.

5. Conclusion
------------------------------------------------------------
Awaiting outcome.

6. What Comes Next
------------------------------------------------------------
Complete the current investigation first.

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that vix30 xlb outperformance 20d long events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when vix closes above 30 (first close in 30-day cluster, market stress spike)
-> buy materials etf xlb at next open, hold 20 trading days. expect xlb to
produce abnormal return vs spy of at least +2.0% over 20 days,
then XLB should rise by approximately 2.3% within 20 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Market stress VIX spikes trigger correlated
deleveraging across risk assets. Sector ETFs more cyclical than broad SPY
(Materials is cyclical-commodity-levered) experience larger drawdown AND
larger recovery bounce. XLB constituents (DOW, FCX, LIN, SHW, NEM) are
leveraged to post-stress reflation via commodity pricing and industrial demand
recovery.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Institutional deleveraging during VIX>30 spikes
sells cyclical exposure most aggressively. Post-spike, same institutions re-
risk into cyclicals first as volatility normalizes.
  - Transmission channel: VIX>30 cluster -> correlated risk-off selling ->
oversold cyclicals -> vol mean reversion -> cyclicals bounce harder than SPY
over 10-20d window.
  - Academic reference: Andersen, Bollerslev, Diebold (2007); Fama-French
(1989): volatility extremes correlate with business-cycle-sensitive asset
outperformance in recovery phases.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 16 historical events across 1 stocks (XLB). All returns
are measured as abnormal returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from
broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: XLB ETF continuously traded since 1998. No
survivorship selection at sector-ETF level.

simultaneously. Bonferroni threshold 0.01 at 5 tests. XLB 20d OOS p=0.005

  event timing: VIX close, execute at next regular open.

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that vix30 eem outperformance 10d long events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when vix closes above 30 (first close in 30-day cluster) -> buy emerging
markets etf eem at next open, hold 10 trading days. expect eem to produce
abnormal return vs spy of at least +2.0% over 10 days,
then EEM should rise by approximately 2.6% within 10 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: EEM is high-beta to global risk appetite.
Market stress VIX spikes cause disproportionate EM selling (cross-border
capital flight, USD strength). As vol mean-reverts, risk-on rotation returns
capital to EM first due to valuation gap. Shorter horizon than XLB because EM
reversion is faster.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Global macro and EM-specific funds cut EM exposure
fastest in risk-off. Post-stress, same pool re-enters EM for carry and
valuation, compressing EM-DM spread.
  - Transmission channel: VIX>30 -> USD strength + capital flight from EM ->
EEM oversold vs SPY -> vol mean reversion -> EM relative reflation over 5-10d.
  - Academic reference: De Bondt & Thaler (1985); Harvey (1995) "Predictable

are measured as abnormal returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from

structure, and strategic plans. Unlike directors who attend quarterly board
meetings, CEO/CFO live in the business daily. When they personally commit
capital via open-market purchases (not option grants or 10b5-1 plans), they
signal undervaluation with the highest-quality information available to any
insider. When 3+ insiders cluster-buy AND the CEO/CFO is among them, this is a
double confirmation: CEO/CFO superior information plus multi-insider
coordination signaling broad agreement. The market takes 3-5 trading days to
fully incorporate this signal as information diffuses gradually through Form 4
filing distribution, media coverage, and institutional model updates —
consistent with post-earnings announcement drift dynamics.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: CEO/CFO are rational economic actors risking
personal capital. Open-market code-P purchases require affirmative decision
under strict pre-clearance and short-swing profit rules. They have information
on the full P&L, not just their committee domain. Purchases are costly
commitments unlike sales which have many liquidity motivations.
  - Transmission channel: Form 4 appears on EDGAR within 2 business days of
trade. Financial media and institutional investors parse SEC data and update
models. Retail attention increases. Price discovery occurs over 3-5 days, not
instantly, because not all participants process the signal simultaneously —
creating the abnormal return window.
  - Academic reference: Cohen, Malloy & Pomorski (2012) 'Decoding Inside
Information', Journal of Finance 67(5): CEO/CFO purchases are 12x more
predictive than other insider purchases. Separating routine from opportunistic
trades and focusing on C-suite officers dramatically improves predictive
power.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest studied 438 historical instances. All returns are measured as
abnormal returns against SPY.

Survivorship bias control: 4.8% event drop rate within acceptable threshold.

defaulting to non-CEO/CFO — this narrows the measured differential, meaning
true advantage is likely larger than observed.

selection. Section 16 excludes sub-10% non-officer holders. Market cap >=
$500M for live trading. Possible sector concentration bias not tested.

  driven signal not regime-dependent.

entry at next open after trigger, exit at 5th trading day close. Pre-

Investigation Report: 9777ef67
============================================================
Status: completed  —  Created 2026-03-23

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that sp500 52w low momentum short events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when an s&p 500 large-cap stock (>500m market cap) first crosses below its
52-week low (after 30+ days above that level), short the stock at next day
open, hold 5 trading days. momentum continuation, not bounce,
then ABT should fall by approximately 1.68% within 5 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Large-cap S&P 500 stocks that first breach
their 52-week low continue declining due to automated stop-loss selling,
institutional risk management exits, and momentum algorithm amplification. The
52-week low is a salient price anchor; once breached, negative momentum
dominates for 1-10 trading days.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Retail investors with stop-loss orders at 52-week
lows are forced sellers. Institutional risk managers must exit per mandates.
Momentum algorithms amplify.
  - Transmission channel: 52-week low breach triggers automated sell orders ->
price continues falling -> momentum algos amplify -> buyers step back from
catching a falling knife for 1-10 days.
  - Academic reference: George & Hwang (2004, JF): 52-Week High and Momentum.
De Bondt & Thaler (1985, JF): shorter-window momentum before 3-5 year
reversal.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 209 historical events across 10 stocks (AAPL, MSFT, INTC,
JPM, BAC, JNJ, NKE, BA and 2 others). All returns are measured as abnormal
returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: S&P 500 large-cap universe. Very low survivorship
bias. Stocks that exit S&P 500 remain tradeable.

Selection bias control: Algorithmic event definition: first breach of 52w low
after 30+ days above. No outcome selection.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  broad market direction: SPY direction tested. Signal works in up, flat, and
  down markets. No filter applied.
  vix level: VIX-conditioned results: calm (-1.71%), elevated (-1.84%), crisis
  (-1.47%). All p<0.01. Signal does not depend on VIX regime.
  sector trend: Signal measured across all sectors. No sector concentration.
  Mechanism is price-anchor-driven.
  survivorship bias: S&P 500 large-cap universe. Very low survivorship bias
  risk. Stocks leaving index remain tradeable.
  selection bias: Algorithmic event definition (first touch after 30-day gap).
  No cherry-picking.
  event timing: Entry at next market open. ABT first crossed 52w low on
  2026-03-20. Entry 2026-03-23 open.
  market regime: Regime-robust: calm -1.71% (N=60, p=0.005), elevated -1.84%
  (N=81, p=0.002), crisis -1.47% (N=68, p=0.012). No VIX gate.

3. Success Criteria (locked before testing)
------------------------------------------------------------
To count as valid: effect appears in at least 62% of instances; abnormal
return exceeds 1.68%; significance survives multiple testing correction.

Confidence: 7/10  |  Pre-registration: 0c907ffae7773594

4. Outcome
------------------------------------------------------------
  Stock return:     -0.41%
  SPY return:       -3.25%
  Abnormal return:  +3.02%
  Direction:        WRONG
  Magnitude ratio:  0.00x of predicted
  Timing:           Full 5-day hold (March 23-27). No downward momentum relative to market.

5. Conclusion
------------------------------------------------------------
The hypothesis is not supported. The stock moved in the opposite direction
from what was predicted.

Mechanism check: No. No momentum continuation below 52w low. ABT was
relatively defensive.

Attribution: ~100% market-driven selloff. ABT benefited from defensive
rotation into healthcare.

What was unexpected: ABT significantly outperformed broad market during this
period — defensive healthcare characteristics overrode the 52w low momentum
signal.

  ABT short OOS test (sp500_52w_low_momentum_short): Stock fell only 0.23% vs
  SPY -3.25%. Abnormal return = +3.02% — ABT outperformed the market
  significantly. The short hypothesis failed. ABT held up as a defensive
  healthcare stock in a broad selloff. Confirms dead end classification.

6. What Comes Next
------------------------------------------------------------
The hypothesis failed. Before generating a new one: can you explain WHY it
failed? Was the causal mechanism wrong, or did a specific condition invalidate
it? A new hypothesis is only warranted if the failure produced a specific,
testable insight.

Warnings

(SHORT when stock removed from index), N=21 events (2021-2026).

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that tariff defensive retail long events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when a major tariff escalation is announced (>10% universal/reciprocal tariff
or expansion to major import categories), buy defensive consumer
staples/discount retailers (cost, wmt, xlp) at next day open, hold 5 trading
days. these stocks outperform as institutional investors rotate out of
cyclicals into defensive sectors during trade war uncertainty,
then COST should rise by approximately 3.57% within 10 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Tariff shock triggers broad market uncertainty
→ institutional portfolio managers de-risk by rotating from cyclical to
defensive sectors → consumer staples (COST, WMT) receive inflows → abnormal
outperformance vs SPY for 5-20 trading days. The effect strengthens over time
(2025 events show +4-11% abnormal returns vs +1-5% in 2018-2019), suggesting
the defensive rotation thesis is increasingly institutionally recognized.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Institutional risk managers reduce cyclical equity
exposure per mandates during trade uncertainty → increase consumer staples
allocation. Retail investors shift spending to discount retailers during
tariff-induced economic uncertainty. Risk parity funds reduce equity beta →
tilt to defensive sectors.
  - Transmission channel: Tariff announcement → broad equity selloff →
defensive rotation into non-cyclicals → WMT/COST/XLP outperform SPY. Takes
1-20 days to fully manifest as institutional rebalancing occurs. Dollar stores
and warehouse clubs benefit from consumer trading down during economic
uncertainty.
  - Academic reference: Novy-Marx (2012) 'The Other Side of Value: The Gross
Profitability Premium' - defensive high-quality stocks outperform cyclicals in
risk-off regimes. Faber (2013) sector rotation models show consumer staples
outperform in late economic cycle/uncertainty. Baker & Wurgler (2006) investor
sentiment risk-off rotation well documented.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 21 historical events across 3 stocks (COST, WMT, XLP).
All returns are measured as abnormal returns against SPY to isolate the event
effect from broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: COST, WMT, XLP all survived full period with market
caps > $50B. Dollar general (DG) also shows the pattern but with more
variance. Failed retailers (Sears, JC Penney) not included - but these were
NOT in the signal universe (we're long defensives, not short failing
retailers). Low survivorship bias risk.

Selection bias control: Tested 9 sectors/stocks simultaneously: ADM, BG, NUE,
STLD, WMT, TGT, DAL, XME, MOO. Only WMT, COST, XLP (consumer staples/defensive
retail) showed the pattern. TGT, DLTR (higher China import exposure) did NOT
show it. This cross-sectional consistency validates the mechanism: defensive
rotation, not random selection.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  broad market direction: Signal holds in both up (2018-03-22: SPY +0.6%) and
  down (2019-05-10: SPY -2.4%) market conditions. 2018-09-18 is the one near-
  miss - low shock value event.
  vix level: Effect present in all VIX regimes (VIX 13-35 range). Stronger
  when VIX spikes more sharply.
  sector trend: Consumer staples sector rotation is the transmission
  mechanism, not baseline trend. WMT/COST lagged SPY before all 7 events but
  outperformed after.
  survivorship bias: COST, WMT, XLP all survived full observation period. Some
  small retailers failed during tariff wars but we use large-caps only.
  selection bias: Tested across 3 instruments simultaneously. Not hand-picked
  - tested alongside TGT, DLTR, BG, ADM, DAL. TGT/DLTR/ag stocks do NOT show
  the same effect, confirming this is specific to defensive warehouse/staples
  not all retail.
  event timing: Using next-day open entry to avoid look-ahead. All tariff
  announcements were after-hours or pre-market.
  market regime: Tested across Obama-era (2009 tire tariffs NOT included -
  only 2018+ Trump era). Signal appears regime-specific to Trump-era tariff
  wars but is consistent within that regime.

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that fda clinical rejection short events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when the fda issues a clinical/efficacy rejection (crl, rtf, or adcomm
negative vote) for a single-pipeline small biotech, causing a 55%+ single-day
crash, short the stock at the open of the crash day and hold for 3 days,
then REPL should fall by approximately 20.0% within 3 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: After a 55%+ crash from an FDA clinical
rejection, institutional holders who were long continue forced selling for 2-5
days as they unwind positions (position limits, redemption pressure, risk
management rules). Day-traders who shorted on crash day take profits within
1-2 days but selling pressure from institutional unwinding dominates. The
stock has near-zero remaining fundamental value for a single-pipeline drug
company post-rejection, so there is no natural buyer base to absorb the
continued selling. Market makers widen spreads and reduce size, amplifying the
downward drift. This institutional forced-selling channel has been documented
in equity event studies (Coval & Stafford 2007 on fire sales).

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors incentives
  - Transmission channel
  - Academic reference

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 6 historical events across 6 stocks (ACER, ARDX, NERV,
GRTX, OTLK, APLT). All returns are measured as abnormal returns against SPY to
isolate the event effect from broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: Delisted tickers (ACER via Tiingo) are INCLUDED in
the analysis. Companies that went to zero after CRL (worst outcomes) are
INCLUDED, making our measured continuation returns potentially conservative
estimates. Favorable survivorship bias direction.

Selection bias control: Events selected by: (1) FDA clinical/efficacy
rejection signal, (2) single-pipeline drug company, (3) >55% single-day crash.

continuation. Additionally, pre-anticipated CRLs (where stock already declined
before announcement) appear to show reversal rather than continuation (ALDX

3). Filter: exclude events where stock declined >20% in the 10 days BEFORE the

------------------------------------------------------------
Given that clinical efficacy failure short events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when short single-pipeline small biotech stock at open the trading day after a
>55% crash caused by phase 2/3 clinical trial failure or fda complete response
letter (crl) with clinical/efficacy deficiency. hold 3 days. qualifying

drug/program — company has no other active clinical programs, (3) market cap
<$500m at time of event, (4) not pre-anticipated — stock not down >20% in

no announced fda meeting plans or subgroup-based nda submissions at time of
event (companies with announced path forward bounce instead of continuing

The proposed causal mechanism: When a single-pipeline small biotech's only
drug fails Phase 2/3 or receives a clinical CRL, all projected revenue
evaporates overnight. The crash is immediate but continuation occurs over 3-5
days because: (1) Institutional portfolio managers require 1-3 days to process
and execute forced exits from biotech holdings that violate their 'viable
revenue path' mandate; (2) The initial gap-down to market open typically
underestimates ultimate fundamental value — the stock's only value was the
pipeline drug, so intrinsic value approaches the cash balance, and the market
takes 2-3 days to price this in; (3) Retail momentum traders (who see the
crash and sell) add downward pressure over days 1-3.

explicitly classified as excluded by single-pipeline filter.

Investigation Report: 7b5d0120
============================================================
Status: superseded  —  Created 2026-03-22

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that insider buying cluster n6to9 events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when 6-9 corporate insiders at the same company buy shares within a short
window, the stock outperforms the market over the next 5 trading days.
discovery (2021-2022, n=173): avg 5d abnormal return +3.57%, pos_rate=59.0%,
p=0.0001. validation (2023-2025, n=178): avg 5d abnormal return +7.80%,
pos_rate=62.9%, p<0.0001. both periods pass multiple testing correction,
then TBD should rise by approximately 3.57% within 5 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Multiple insiders at the same firm each
independently filing Form 4 purchases signals broad internal conviction. At
6-9 insiders, the cluster exceeds random coincidence but remains below the
n>=10 level where reporting artifacts and lock-up expirations inflate counts.
Market participants take 3-10 days to aggregate Form 4 data across filings, so
the signal is slow to be arbitraged. Price drifts upward as the aggregated
signal propagates through data vendors and quant fund scanners.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Corporate insiders (officers, directors) risk
personal capital when buying shares. A cluster of 6-9 individuals each
independently placing buy orders signals high internal confidence — they bear
downside risk personally and face SEC scrutiny if trading on MNPI, so
frivolous or misleading buying is disincentivized.
  - Transmission channel: EDGAR Form 4 filings become public within 2 business
days of the trade. Data aggregators (OpenInsider, SEC bulk feeds) surface
clusters. Informed investors and quant funds scan these feeds, generating buy
pressure as the signal propagates. Price drift occurs over 5-10 days as the
aggregated signal reaches more market participants.
  - Academic reference: Seyhun (1998) Investment Intelligence from Insider
Trading documents insider buying predicts positive abnormal returns. Cohen,
Malloy & Pomorski (2012) Decoding Inside Information (Journal of Finance) show
opportunistic insider buys generating 6%+ abnormal returns over 1-6 months.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest studied 173 historical instances. All returns are measured as
abnormal returns against SPY.

Survivorship bias control: Discovery sample from EDGAR bulk data includes
delisted tickers. yfinance failures for delisted symbols reduce measurable
events (216 in CSV, 173 measured in discovery; 221 in CSV, 178 measured in
validation). Surviving firms are likely outperformers — true effect size may
be smaller for real-time trading where some targets later delist.

Selection bias control: Cluster detection requires n_insiders 6-9 in a rolling
window. Very active filers (e.g., post-IPO lock-up expirations) may inflate
cluster counts artificially. SPAC-related tickers (LMACA, ROCRU, ZTAQU in 2021
sample) have unusual insider dynamics. The signal may be driven by a subset of
high-quality clusters that are hard to identify ex-ante.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  market regime: Backtested across 2021-2022 (bull + rising rate environment)
  and validated on 2023-2025 (mixed/recovery). Validation abnormal returns are
  substantially higher (7.8% vs 3.57% at 5d), confirming the effect is not a
  2021 bull-market artifact.
  broad market direction: Discovery period includes both bullish 2021 and
  bearish 2022. Validation covers 2023-2025 recovery and mild bull. The signal
  strengthened in validation despite varied broad market conditions,
  suggesting it is not direction-dependent.
  vix level: Prior regime analysis (cluster_with_regime.csv) shows the n=6-9
  signal is robust across VIX regimes. Effect present in both calm (<20) and
  elevated (20-30) VIX. No explicit VIX filter applied in this backtest.
  sector trend: Events span diverse sectors (biotech, industrials, financials,
  tech, consumer). No sector ETF adjustment applied in this backtest. Sector-
  adjusted analysis is a future research task — effect could be partially
  sector-trend driven.
  survivorship bias: EDGAR bulk data includes delisted tickers.
  yfinance/Tiingo failures for delisted symbols drop ~20% of events. Surviving
  firms likely outperformers. True live-trading effect may be smaller. Noted
  as primary bias risk.
  selection bias: Cluster detection requires n_insiders=6-9 in a rolling
  window. Post-IPO lock-up expirations and SPAC structures may inflate cluster
  counts for a subset of events. Signal may be driven by a subset of high-
  quality clusters hard to identify ex-ante.
  event timing: Form 4 filings become public 1-2 business days after the
  trade. Entry is at next open (entry_price="open"). Some delay between filing
  and data aggregator surfacing introduces timing uncertainty. Effect measured
  from open after cluster detection.

3. Success Criteria (locked before testing)
------------------------------------------------------------
To count as valid: effect appears in at least 59% of instances; abnormal
return exceeds 3.57%; significance survives multiple testing correction.

Confidence: 4/10  |  Pre-registration: 14459fae86460b7f

hypotheses.report:
Investigation Report: 9f8a4c61
============================================================
Status: superseded  —  Created 2026-03-22

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that insider cluster 6 to 9 events have historically produced abnormal
returns in affected stocks,
when 6-9 corporate insiders (officers/directors) make open-market stock
purchases within 30 days of each other (each >$50k), the stock outperforms the
market over the following 3 trading days. this is the optimal cluster size
tier: regime-robust (works in both calm and elevated vix) unlike 3-5 clusters,
and signal-clean (unlike 10+ clusters which are noise),
then TBD should rise by approximately 3.48% within 3 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: Strong insider conviction signal. 6-9
coordinated buyers indicates management expects imminent positive catalyst.
Unlike 3-5 insiders (fails in elevated VIX p=0.27), 6-9 insiders overcome
market noise even in volatile regimes (p=0.027 in VIX 25-30). Unlike 10+
insiders (coin flip p=0.64 -- contaminated by compensation plans and SPAC
mechanics), 6-9 represents genuine coordinated conviction. Entry: long at next
open after last cluster filing. Exit: 3 trading days later.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Officers and directors risk own capital in open-
market purchases. Form 4 disclosure within 2 days creates accountability. When
6-9 different insiders independently buy, each faces legal risk from trading
on material non-public information. 6-9 threshold filters most 10b5-1 pre-
planned transactions and compensation plan activity.
  - Transmission channel: Multiple-insider buying signals positive
expectations to the market. Investors monitoring SEC EDGAR Form 4 filings
detect the pattern and trade on it, transmitting the signal to prices. Effect
peaks at 3-5 days as institutional investors complete position-building.
  - Academic reference: Kang, Kim & Wang (2018): cluster purchases yield 3.8%
abnormal return over 21 days. Lakonishok & Lee (2001): insider purchases
strongly predictive. Seyhun (1998): insider buying clusters particularly
informative. Extended to 2021-2025 confirming persistence.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 132 historical events across 7 stocks (KFS, CODI, GTX,
DFH, NOG, BLCO, GO). All returns are measured as abnormal returns against SPY
to isolate the event effect from broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: Discovery (2021-2022): 39% measurement failure due
to delisted tickers (SPAC-era companies, micro-cap acquisitions). Exceeds 30%
threshold but failures are predominantly acquired companies (positive
survivorship bias). Validation (2023-2025): <1% failure rate, clean data.

Selection bias control: Events selected from SEC quarterly bulk data
(objective). The 6-9 sub-tier is a data-snooped refinement discovered through
tier analysis. Mitigated by strict temporal OOS: discovery closed before
validation began.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  broad market direction: SPY 5d prior trend tested as filter -- NOT
  predictive (r=0.030, p=0.23). Signal works regardless of recent SPY
  direction.
  vix level: VIX is the primary regime factor (r=-0.096, p<0.001). n=6-9
  remains significant in BOTH calm VIX<20 (p=0.0004) AND elevated VIX 20-30
  (p=0.027). Best returns in VIX<15 (+5.70% EV) through weakest in VIX 25-30
  (+2.37% EV). Trade when VIX<30.
  sector trend: Signal measured across all sectors with no sector filter.
  Mechanism is insider-conviction-driven, not sector momentum. No sector
  concentration observed.
  survivorship bias: Discovery (2021-2022): 39% failure due to delisted
  tickers (SPAC-era, micro-cap acquisitions). Failures predominantly acquired
  companies (positive bias: acquirees receive premium). Validation
  (2023-2025): <1% failure rate. Net: favorable bias.
  selection bias: Events from SEC quarterly bulk data (objective). Open-market
  purchases only (code P), each >$50K, within 30-day window. The 6-9 sub-tier
  is a data-snooped refinement -- mitigated by strict temporal OOS split.
  event timing: Entry at next market open after cluster detection. 30-day
  window means filing can span a month. No same-day entries -- always next-
  day-open to avoid look-ahead bias.
  market regime: Tested across 2021-2025 including bull (2021, 2023-2025),
  bear (2022), and elevated volatility (March 2020-type tariff shock). Signal
  persists across regimes for n=6-9 tier.

3. Success Criteria (locked before testing)
------------------------------------------------------------
To count as valid: effect appears in at least 70% of instances; abnormal
return exceeds 3.48%; significance survives multiple testing correction.

Confidence: 7/10  |  Pre-registration: a1045afc924896b8

4. Outcome
------------------------------------------------------------
Not yet tested.

5. Conclusion
------------------------------------------------------------
Awaiting outcome.

6. What Comes Next
------------------------------------------------------------
Complete the current investigation first.

------------------------------------------------------------
- Symbol is TBD — must be resolved to a real ticker before activation.

Status: abandoned  —  Created 2026-03-23

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that first ig credit upgrade events have historically produced abnormal
returns in affected stocks,
when a large-cap company (>$1b market cap) receives its first investment-grade
credit rating from any major rating agency (moody's baa3, s&p bbb-, or fitch
bbb-), buy at the open of the next trading day and hold for 3 trading days.
qualifying: (1) company has never had ig status from this agency before, or
previously lost ig and is now regaining it; (2) upgrade is not accompanied by
same-day m&a or earnings release; (3) no other agency already upgraded this
company to ig in the preceding 10 trading days. additional exclusion
criterion: stock must not have risen more than 250% in the 24 months prior to
the upgrade date (extreme pre-run indicates equity market already priced in
credit improvement, neutralizing the signal),
then TBD should rise by approximately 2.42% within 3 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: A first IG upgrade removes a company from high-
yield bond indices and causes buying pressure from IG-mandated investors. IG
bond fund managers must purchase new IG issuers to track IG indices (Bloomberg
Agg, LQD). The company gains lower-cost debt access, reducing interest burden
and improving equity value. Institutional investors use credit ratings as a
quality proxy -- an upgrade signals improved balance sheet health. The
mechanism is strongest for FIRST IG upgrades: follow-on upgrades show flat-to-
negative returns (n=3: avg 5d=-2.06%), confirming the market prices subsequent
agency upgrades after the first.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: IG-mandated bond fund managers must buy new IG
issuers for index compliance. Equity analysts re-rate fundamental quality.
Retail investors use credit ratings as quality signal.
  - Transmission channel: Bond fund inflows reduce yield spreads and lower
cost of capital, increasing equity DCF valuation. Stock price benefits through
(a) lower discount rate, (b) improved EPS from lower interest expense, (c)
positive sentiment from quality signal.
  - Academic reference: Kliger and Sarig (2000, JF): credit rating upgrades
cause significant positive equity returns. Hand, Holthausen and Leftwich
(1992, JF): IG vs non-IG threshold crossings have larger equity impact.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------
The backtest covered 19 historical events across 12 stocks (DAL, CNO, TECK,
TMUS, ALLY, NFLX, FCX, M and 4 others). All returns are measured as abnormal
returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from broad market moves.

Survivorship bias control: IG upgrades are public events with clear
announcement dates. Companies receiving IG upgrades are by definition still
listed. Excluded companies would likely be acquisitions at premium, making
survivorship bias conservative -- our measured returns may understate the true
effect.

Selection bias control: Events were selected via web search for known rising
star upgrades, biasing toward well-publicized large-cap events. Exclusion
criteria (no concurrent M&A, no same-day earnings, no follow-on upgrades) are
necessary to isolate the credit upgrade effect but reduce sample size.
Systematic data access would enable a complete unbiased dataset.

Confounders recorded at entry:
  broad market direction: Mixed: 8 of 12 events in rising markets, 4 in
  flat/declining markets. No broad market filter applied.
  vix level: Mix of calm (2016, 2019, 2021) and elevated (Oct 2018, early
  2022) VIX environments. Signal present in both.
  sector trend: Diverse sectors: airlines (DAL), finance (CNO, ALLY, MSCI),
  tech (TMUS, NFLX), materials (FCX, TECK), retail (M), auto (F), ride-share
  (UBER). Cross-sector diversity reduces sector-trend confound.
  survivorship bias: Events are public announcements; companies still listed
  post-upgrade. Delisted companies post-upgrade likely acquisitions at
  premium, making bias conservative.
  selection bias: Events selected via web search for known rising stars.
  Biases toward large-cap, well-publicized events. Systematic
  Bloomberg/Moody's data would give complete unbiased sample.
  event timing: All credit upgrades announced pre-market or after-hours. Entry
  at next open (day+1 open), exiting at close of day+3.
  market regime: Tested across bull (2016, 2019, 2021, 2023-24) and correction
  markets (Oct 2018, early 2022). Signal appears regime-independent across
  8-year span.
  pre event equity runup: Analyzed. 24m pre-event run-up has r=-0.404
  correlation with 3d abnormal returns. 12m window has r=-0.045 (not useful).
  Events with 24m run-up >250% should be excluded as the market has already
  priced in credit improvement. Filter validated: all 12 discovery events have
  24m run-up <200%. VST (403% 24m) is the negative OOS case.
  announcement timing: CRITICAL: after-hours announcements only — during-hours
  upgrades are priced in before next open; see CCL Oct 2025 case

1. Hypothesis
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that sp500 52w low catalyst short events have historically produced
abnormal returns in affected stocks,
when an s&p 500 large-cap stock first crosses its 52-week low and experiences
>2% abnormal decline on day 1 (confirming a fundamental catalyst — earnings
miss, guidance cut, or major negative news), the stock continues declining.
this is a refinement of the sp500_52w_low_momentum_short signal targeting only
high-conviction catalyst-driven breakdowns,
then TBD should fall by approximately 4.44% within 5 trading days.

The proposed causal mechanism: When a large-cap stock breaks its 52w low due
to a fundamental catalyst (not just technical), the causal chain is stronger:
(1) Earnings/news shocks reveal genuine business deterioration, not just
noise, (2) Institutional holders reduce positions over multiple sessions
(cannot exit all at once without market impact), (3) Momentum algos confirm
the signal and add short pressure, (4) Stop-losses cascade as the large d1
drop triggers additional automated selling. The combination of informational
shock + technical breakdown creates persistent negative momentum over 5 days.

Mechanism evidence:
  - Actors and incentives: Institutional holders must reduce positions
gradually after large negative catalyst (earnings miss reveals deteriorating
fundamentals). CTAs/quant funds go short on new 52w lows with large momentum
signal. Index funds may also reduce overweight positions.
  - Transmission channel: Large catalyst creates information-driven repricing
that takes multiple days to complete as institutional selling unfolds. Retail
stop-losses trigger at 52w low. Short sellers add positions after
confirmation.
  - Academic reference: Ball & Brown (1968, JAR): Post-Earnings Announcement
Drift (PEAD) — earnings information takes 5-10 days to be fully priced in.
George & Hwang (2004, JF): 52-week extremes as anchoring points. Bernard &
Thomas (1989, JAR): institutional underreaction to earnings information.

2. Test Design
------------------------------------------------------------

JPM, BAC, JNJ, NKE, BA and 2 others). All returns are measured as abnormal
returns against SPY to isolate the event effect from broad market moves.

bias. All stocks have market cap >500M at time of trade.

Selection bias control: Fully algorithmic entry condition: (1) first 52w low
close after 30+ day gap, AND (2) day-1 abnormal return < -2%. No subjective
filter.

  tech. No sector concentration — signal is broad market phenomenon.
  survivorship bias: S&P 500 large-cap universe (>500M market cap). Very low
  survivorship bias. All stocks are current or former S&P 500 members.
  selection bias: Entry condition (52w low + >2% abnormal d1 drop) is fully
  algorithmic. No subjective filter. Catalyst events are naturally selected by
  price action, not by researcher.
  event timing: Enter at open of day 2 after confirming day-1 large drop, OR
